request.description:
## Create User

Creates a new user in the system by accepting a name and email address. On success, returns the newly created user's details along with a system-assigned ID.

---

### Request Body

The request body must be sent as `application/json`.

| Field   | Type   | Required | Description                        |
|---------|--------|----------|------------------------------------|
| `name`  | string | Yes      | The full name of the user to create |
| `email` | string | Yes      | The email address of the new user  |

**Example:**
```json
{
  "name": "Jane Doe",
  "email": "janedoe@example.com"
}
```

---

### Response

**Status:** `201 Created`

Returns a success flag, a message, and the created user's data.

| Field              | Type    | Description                              |
|--------------------|---------|------------------------------------------|
| `success`          | boolean | Indicates whether the request succeeded  |
| `message`          | string  | A human-readable status message          |
| `data.id`          | integer | The system-assigned ID of the new user   |
| `data.name`        | string  | The name of the created user             |
| `data.email`       | string  | The email address of the created user    |

**Example:**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "User created successfully.",
  "data": {
    "id": 7,
    "name": "Jane Doe",
    "email": "janedoe@example.com"
  }
}
```

query.description: Search user by name or email

request.description:
## Get Paginated Users

Fetches a paginated list of all registered users in the system.

**Method:** `GET`  
**URL:** `{{BASE_URL}}/users`

---

### Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `BASE_URL` | The base URL of the API server (e.g., `https://api.example.com`) |

---

### Response Structure

A successful response returns a `200 OK` status with the following JSON structure:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Users fetched successfully.",
  "data": {
    "meta": {
      "total": 1,
      "page": 1,
      "limit": 10
    },
    "data": [
      {
        "id": 2,
        "name": "John Doe",
        "email": "johndoe@example.com"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `success` | `boolean` | Indicates whether the request was successful |
| `message` | `string` | A human-readable status message |
| `data.meta.total` | `number` | Total number of users available |
| `data.meta.page` | `number` | Current page number |
| `data.meta.limit` | `number` | Maximum number of users returned per page |
| `data.data` | `array` | Array of user objects for the current page |
| `data.data[].id` | `number` | Unique identifier of the user |
| `data.data[].name` | `string` | Full name of the user |
| `data.data[].email` | `string` | Email address of the user |

query.description: search by event title or description

request.description:
## Create Event

Creates a new event in the system.

**Method:** `POST`  
**URL:** `{{BASE_URL}}/events`

---

### Request Body

Send a JSON object with the following fields:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `title` | string | Yes | The title of the event |
| `description` | string | No | A detailed description of the event |
| `date` | string (ISO 8601) | Yes | The date of the event (e.g. `2026-03-29`) |
| `totalCapacity` | integer | Yes | The maximum number of attendees allowed |

**Example:**

``` json
{
  "title": "Project Hail Mary",
  "description": "Science teacher Ryland Grace wakes up on...",
  "date": "2026-03-29",
  "totalCapacity": 5
}

 ```

---

### Responses

#### `201 Created`

Returned when the event is successfully created.

``` json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Event created successfully.",
  "data": {
    "id": 1,
    "title": "Project Hail Mary",
    "description": "Science teacher Ryland Grace wakes up on...",
    "date": "2026-03-29T00:00:00.000Z",
    "totalCapacity": 5,
    "remainingTickets": 5
  }
}

 ```

**Response fields:**

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `success` | boolean | Indicates whether the request was successful |
| `message` | string | A human-readable status message |
| `data.id` | integer | The unique identifier of the created event |
| `data.title` | string | The title of the event |
| `data.description` | string | The description of the event |
| `data.date` | string (ISO 8601) | The event date in UTC format |
| `data.totalCapacity` | integer | The total number of seats available |
| `data.remainingTickets` | integer | The number of tickets still available (equals `totalCapacity` on creation) |

## Mark Event Attendance

Marks attendance for a specific event using a valid ticket/attendance code. This endpoint validates the provided code and records the user's entry for the event.

---

### Path Variable

| Variable | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `id` | integer | The unique ID of the event for which attendance is being marked. |

---

### Request Body

**Content-Type:** `application/json`

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `code` | string (UUID) | Yes | The attendance or ticket code associated with the user's booking for the event. |

**Example:**
```json
{
  "code": "b96ac02d-7216-4929-9070-563cdc72cfd1"
}
```

---

### Response

#### 200 OK — Attendance marked successfully

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `success` | boolean | Indicates whether the request was successful. |
| `message` | string | A human-readable status message. |
| `data.attendanceId` | integer | The unique ID of the attendance record created. |
| `data.userId` | integer | The ID of the user whose attendance was recorded. |
| `data.eventId` | integer | The ID of the event for which attendance was marked. |
| `data.ticketsBooked` | integer | The number of tickets booked by the user for this event. |
| `data.entryTime` | string (ISO 8601) | The timestamp at which the user's entry was recorded. |

**Example:**
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Event attendance marked successfully.",
  "data": {
    "attendanceId": 2,
    "userId": 7,
    "eventId": 1,
    "ticketsBooked": 1,
    "entryTime": "2026-03-29T13:18:25.208Z"
  }
}
```

## Get Paginated Events

Fetches a paginated list of upcoming events. Results are ordered and limited based on pagination parameters.

---

### Response Structure

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | boolean | Indicates whether the request was successful |
| `message` | string | A human-readable status message |
| `data` | object | Contains pagination metadata and the list of events |

#### `data.meta`

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `total` | integer | Total number of upcoming events available |
| `page` | integer | Current page number |
| `limit` | integer | Maximum number of events returned per page |

#### `data.data` — Event Object

| Field | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | integer | Unique identifier for the event |
| `title` | string | Title of the event |
| `description` | string | Detailed description of the event |
| `date` | string (ISO 8601) | Scheduled date and time of the event (e.g. `2026-03-30T00:00:00.000Z`) |
| `totalCapacity` | integer | Total number of tickets available for the event |
| `remainingTickets` | integer | Number of tickets still available for booking |

---

### Example Response

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Upcoming events fetched successfully.",
  "data": {
    "meta": {
      "total": 1,
      "page": 1,
      "limit": 10
    },
    "data": [
      {
        "id": 2,
        "title": "Project Hail Mary",
        "description": "Science teacher Ryland Grace wakes up on...",
        "date": "2026-03-30T00:00:00.000Z",
        "totalCapacity": 5,
        "remainingTickets": 5
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

## Create Booking

Creates a new booking for a user against a specific event. On success, the API returns the booking details including a unique booking code and the scheduled booking date.

---

### Request Body

The request body must be sent as raw JSON with `Content-Type: application/json`.

| Parameter | Type    | Required | Description                          |
|-----------|---------|----------|--------------------------------------|
| `userId`  | integer | Yes      | The ID of the user making the booking |
| `eventId` | integer | Yes      | The ID of the event to book           |

**Example Request Body**
```json
{
  "userId": 2,
  "eventId": 2
}
```

---

### Example Response

**Status:** `201 Created`

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Booking created successfully.",
  "data": {
    "id": 3,
    "userId": 2,
    "eventId": 2,
    "bookingDate": "2026-03-29T13:14:33.410Z",
    "code": "e3a37db4-fb85-4afd-a617-b55fd1374a90"
  }
}
```

#### Response Fields

| Field               | Type    | Description                                      |
|---------------------|---------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `success`           | boolean | Indicates whether the request was successful     |
| `message`           | string  | A human-readable status message                  |
| `data.id`           | integer | Unique identifier of the created booking         |
| `data.userId`       | integer | ID of the user who made the booking              |
| `data.eventId`      | integer | ID of the event that was booked                  |
| `data.bookingDate`  | string  | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the booking was made  |
| `data.code`         | string  | Unique UUID code assigned to this booking        |
